title: Pastoral
content:
If it were only still!--
             With far away the shrill
             Crying of a cock;
             Or the shaken bell
             From a cow's throat
             Moving through the bushes;
             Or the soft shock
             Of wizened apples falling
             From an old tree
             In a forgotten orchard
             Upon the hilly rock!

             Oh, grey hill,
             Where the grazing herd
             Licks the purple blossom,
             Crops the spiky weed!
             Oh, stony pasture,
             Where the tall mullein
             Stands up so sturdy
             On its little seed!
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
